findings.description: src/assistant/core/graph_client.py:1047 re-enters the 401 invalid_token force-refresh branch after a 302/307 download redirect; line 1086 then re-adds Authorization to the current redirected URL, so a redirected HTTPS host that returns WWW-Authenticate: invalid_token can receive the Graph bearer token.
findings.resolution: Gate the force-refresh branch to pre-redirect Graph requests only, for example require redirect_follows == 0 or a trusted Graph host before refreshing/re-attaching Authorization. After a redirect, treat 401 as a normal download failure and add a regression test asserting no bearer is sent to the redirected host.

findings.description: src/assistant/core/graph_client.py:516 still catches only selected httpx transport errors, not all httpx.TransportError subclasses; ReadError, WriteError, CloseError, LocalProtocolError, ProxyError, and UnsupportedProtocol can still escape raw, so R6 is not fully addressed.
findings.resolution: Catch httpx.TransportError in _send_with_auth_retry, emit trace_graph_call for every subclass, and map known subclasses to distinct error_code values with a transport_error fallback.

findings.description: src/assistant/core/resilience.py:589 sets current_retry_attempt without resetting it; after a retried call completes, later non-retrying POSTs in the same task read a stale retry_attempt via src/assistant/core/graph_client.py:509.
findings.resolution: Store the ContextVar token returned by current_retry_attempt.set(...) and reset it in a finally block around each attempt, or otherwise restore the previous value before resilient_http returns or raises.

findings.description: src/assistant/core/graph_client.py:1095 raises GraphAPIError for an invalid download redirect before emitting trace_graph_call, leaving the redirect-rejection path without a per-request span.
findings.resolution: Emit trace_graph_call with status_code=response.status_code, bytes_streamed=0, and error="GraphAPIError" before raising the redirect rejection error.
